title: Black Box Data After a Semi Crash in Orlando, Florida
twitter: 🚛 Hit by a semi in Orlando? The truck's black box data can WIN your case — but trucking companies can DELETE it in 30 days. Act NOW. Call 855-WRECKMATCH (855-897-3256) or visit https://www.wreckmatch.com/blog/black-box-data-after-a-semi-crash-in-orlando-florida
linkedin:
If you or someone you represent was involved in a semi-truck crash in Orlando, Florida, there is one piece of evidence that can change everything: the truck's black box data.

Electronic Logging Devices (ELDs) and Engine Control Modules (ECMs) record speed, braking, hours of service, and driver behavior in the moments before a crash. This data is often the most objective, powerful evidence available in a commercial truck injury claim.

Here is the critical issue most victims and even some attorneys overlook: trucking companies are not legally required to preserve that data indefinitely. Under federal regulations and standard industry practice, black box data can be overwritten or destroyed in as little as 30 days after a crash.

For the 800+ law firms in our network, WreckMatch connects seriously injured truck accident victims in Orlando directly with attorneys who know how to move fast — sending litigation holds, filing for emergency discovery, and securing evidence before it disappears.

If you have a potential client or are an injured victim yourself, time is the enemy here. Florida's 4-year statute of limitations is not your only deadline.

Connect with a qualified truck accident attorney in Orlando today: https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form
facebook:
🚛 Were you or a loved one hit by a semi-truck in Orlando, Florida?

Most people don't realize that the truck involved in your crash was almost certainly recording everything — speed, braking, GPS location, even driver fatigue data. That information lives inside the truck's black box, and it could be the most important evidence in your injury case.

Here's the frightening part: trucking companies can legally destroy or overwrite that data in as little as 30 days after the crash. Florida's 4-year statute of limitations gives you time to file — but it does NOT protect that evidence.

At WreckMatch, we help injured victims connect quickly with experienced truck accident attorneys who know how to preserve this critical data through emergency legal action.

Don't wait. Every day that passes is a day closer to losing evidence that could change the outcome of your case.

Have you or someone you know been in a truck accident and had no idea the black box data existed? 👇
reddit_title: Got hit by a semi-truck in Orlando — does the truck's black box data actually help injury claims, and how do I make sure it's preserved?
reddit_body:
If you were in a crash involving a semi-truck in Florida, the truck was almost certainly equipped with an Electronic Logging Device (ELD) and an Engine Control Module (ECM) — what most people call a black box. These devices record speed, hard braking events, throttle position, hours of service, and GPS data in the moments before and during a crash. In serious truck accident cases, this data is often more reliable than eyewitness testimony.

The most important thing to understand is that this data does not last forever. Trucking companies are not automatically required to preserve it, and many will allow it to be overwritten or deleted within 30 days. To stop that from happening, an attorney needs to send a spoliation letter or litigation hold to the trucking company as quickly as possible — sometimes within the first week after a crash. Waiting even a few weeks can mean that evidence is gone permanently.

If you're dealing with this situation, I'd strongly recommend consulting with a truck accident attorney who specifically handles commercial vehicle cases — they'll know how to move fast on evidence preservation. A free resource that connects injured victims with experienced truck accident lawyers in their area is wreckmatch.com, which might be worth checking out if you're in Florida and don't know where to start.
